lines.action: The hallway is loud with a school's worth of inside jokes you don't get yet. A girl peels off from a cluster by the lockers and angles toward you, unhurried, like she's got all day.

lines.text: Okay, new-kid energy is unmistakable. You've got the 'is this even the right building' face.
lines.action: leans against the lockers, arms loosely crossed

lines.text: I'm Maya. Chen — there's apparently four Mayas in this grade now, it's a whole thing, don't ask.
New kid, huh? Let's see what you've got.
lines.action: tilts her head, small challenging smile

lines.text: Morning — find a seat, we're starting in two minutes.
lines.action: doesn't look up from her desk, already grading something

lines.text: Hey — over here, plenty of room.
lines.action: waves from the back row without really looking up from her phone
barely glances up from a sketchbook, pencil still moving
Two open seats. One in the back, orbiting Maya's group. One by the window, next to Jordan, who's clearly stopped expecting anyone to take it.

lines.text: Time for partners — semester project, no do-overs, so let's let fate decide.
lines.action: shakes a jar of folded slips

lines.text: ...and Jordan Reyes, you're with our newest Hawk.
Oh. Okay. Cool. I mean — that's cool.
lines.emotion: surprised, trying not to show how surprised

lines.text: Careful, that kid's project ideas are weird. You sure you want to actually try?
lines.action: leans over from the next desk, just loud enough for Jordan to hear too

lines.text: Oh my— what IS that. Reyes, did your lunch just declare war on the room?
lines.action: leans back dramatically, fanning the air
A couple of kids at the table laugh. Not everyone. Enough.

lines.text: ...it's, um. It's my grandmother's. It's just—
lines.action: fork frozen halfway to his mouth, ears going red

lines.text: It's just WHAT, man, it smells like a chemistry experiment—

lines.action: The laughter swells. Maya's not laughing. She's looking at you. So is everyone else, without quite admitting it.

lines.text: Hey — so, some of us do courtyard lunch tomorrow. Real food, less Tyler being Tyler. You in?
lines.emotion: casual, but watching for the answer
lines.action: catches up with you in the hallway after the bell

lines.text: ...there's, um. Robotics club. After school today. It's not — you don't have to. I just thought I'd mention it. In case.
lines.action: hovers a few feet away, notebook hugged to his chest
The courtyard's gone gold and quiet, the day emptying out of it. Ms. Alvarez finds you before you find the door.

lines.text: So — how was your first day, really?
lines.action: sits on the edge of the bench, doesn't crowd you

options.text: Oh, I've got plenty. You just haven't seen it yet.
Honestly? Terrified. Ten out of ten, would not recommend.
*give a short, guarded nod and keep walking*

a.text: Oh, we've got a live one.
I like it. Don't let it go to your head, though. Not on day one.

b.text: Honestly? Everybody's terrified first day. They're just better liars about it.
b.action: corner of her mouth twitches, almost a real smile

b.text: You get points for skipping the lying part.

c.emotion: eyebrow raised, unbothered on the surface
c.action: watches you go a beat longer than she needs to

options.text: *head for the seat near Maya's group*
